title: Soundtrack to 8,000 Job Cuts: A Meta Worker’s Layoff-Themed A.I. Songs
description: On a dark day of layoffs at Meta, one employee responded by creating an internal radio station that plays songs about job cuts — generated by artificial intelligence, of course.
author: Mike Isaac

title: Nvidia’s Profit Hits $58.3 Billion as A.I. Boom Gathers More Steam
description: The chip maker said its profit in its most recent quarter jumped 211 percent from a year earlier thanks to extreme demand from other big technology companies.
author: Tripp Mickle

title: Musk’s SpaceX Reveals Its Finances for the First Time as It Readies for IPO
description: Mr. Musk’s rocket and satellite maker disclosed its financial performance as it prepares to go public in what is set to be one of the largest offerings to date.
author: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch

title: OpenAI Prepares to File for an I.P.O. in Coming Weeks
description: OpenAI would be one of the most highly anticipated potential initial public offerings, in what is set to be a major year of I.P.O.s for Silicon Valley.
author: Mike Isaac and Cade Metz

title: Why ‘Smart’ Products Have Started to Look Like the Dumb Choice
description: How Wi-Fi-connecting, app-based tech led to a backlash in the name of simplicity.
author: Nitsuh Abebe

title: Meta Lays Off 8,000 Employees, as A.I. Casualties Mount
description: Employees have signed petitions against being tracked by A.I. and were trying to figure out who had been let go on Wednesday, as the Silicon Valley giant tries to transform into an A.I.-first company.
author: Eli Tan, Kalley Huang and Mike Isaac

title: How Google Is Starting to Win the A.I. Race
description: Despite its early stumbles, Google’s Gemini has leapfrogged ChatGPT in relevance and usefulness. Soon, it will be ubiquitous.
author: Brian X. Chen

title: Powered by A.I., Google Changes Its Search Box for the First Time in 25 Years
description: Using a new Gemini A.I. model, the tech giant is overhauling its search box dimensions to answer longer queries, adding a video-generation tool and simplifying online shopping.
author: Tripp Mickle, Kate Conger and Brian X. Chen

title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
description: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward computer security and individual digital privacy. He also developed solutions.
author: John Markoff

title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
description: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.
author: Ivan Penn

title: Senate Republicans Introduce Funding Measure to Combat Online Child Abuse
description: As part of the reconciliation bill, the senators have allocated $108 million over three years to add nearly 200 specialists at the Department of Homeland Security.
author: Cecilia Kang

title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
description: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.
author: Jack Ewing and Gabriela Bhaskar

title: As OpenAI Celebrates Court Win Against Musk, Other Challenges Lie Ahead
description: A jury’s rejection of Elon Musk’s $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI was a major hurdle crossed. But the maker of ChatGPT faces a list of other problems.
author: Cade Metz and Natallie Rocha

title: Bipartisan Bill Would Impose New Annual Fee on Electric Vehicles
description: A House transportation bill introduced this week would require owners of electric cars to pay $130 to cover the cost of road repairs.
author: Jack Ewing

title: Dozens of Polymarket Bets Show Signs of Insider Trading, The Times Finds
description: Dozens of long-shot bets on Polymarket, from the war with Iran to the cryptocurrency market, have defied the odds, according to a New York Times examination.
author: Stuart A. Thompson and David Yaffe-Bellany

title: Meta Reassigns 7,000 Employees to Focus on A.I.
description: The company announced the changes two days before it plans to lay off 10 percent of its work force, or about 8,000 employees.
author: Eli Tan

title: Five Takeaways From the Blockbuster Trial Pitting Elon Musk Against OpenAI
description: It took a jury less than two hours to decide that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. But the testimony over three weeks was still illuminating.
author: Cade Metz

title: After Elon Musk’s Court Loss Comes the Long Hot A.I. Summer
description: Even as protests increase, the collapse of Mr. Musk’s suit against OpenAI and Sam Altman will speed up the artificial intelligence juggernaut.
author: David Streitfeld and Natallie Rocha

title: Elon Musk Loses $150 Billion Suit Against OpenAI and Sam Altman
description: A nine-member jury found that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. The setback for the tech mogul frees OpenAI to continue in the artificial intelligence race.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
